strategy: At Round 1 he casts Equalize, capping all damage at 7,999, so you cannot one-shot him; plan for at least two hits.
He recasts his Global if you try to replace it and Cleanse-Charms himself if you Dispel him, so do not waste turns fighting his Global or dispelling.
Join before the second Round; a late wizard gets hit with Shadow Queen Calypso.
He is stunable and beguilable, resists Fire, Myth, and Life 70%, and takes 5% more from Ice, Storm, and Death, so bring Ice, Storm, or Death and use a stun to buy a setup Round.
Because of the 7,999 damage cap, build two solid hits rather than one giant one, and heal through his Fire, Myth, and Life natural attacks.
cheatNotes: A wizard who joins after the second Round is interrupt-cast with Shadow Queen Calypso.
At Round 1 he interrupt-casts Equalize, capping all damage at 7,999.
If you try to replace his Global, he recasts it.
If you Dispel him, he casts a Sun Cleanse Charm on himself to clear it.
